description: raspi-config is the primary configuration tool for Raspberry Pi OS. While normally an interactive CLI GUI, its non-interactive mode allows deep systemic automation and structural changes.

commands.explanation: Launches the standard curses-based interactive graphical interface for adjusting core system configuration settings.
Forces the filesystem to automatically expand and consume all available unallocated space on the MicroSD card upon the next boot sequence.
Silently enables the I2C hardware bus natively in the boot configuration (0 means Enable, 1 means Disable in nonint conventions).
Silently enables the SPI (Serial Peripheral Interface) hardware bus for high-speed sensor communication.
Configures the GPIO Serial Port. (0=Shell/Hardware enabled, 1=Shell/Hardware disabled, 2=Shell disabled/Hardware enabled).
Enables the SSH daemon seamlessly, ensuring headless network administration is permitted.
Instantiates and enables the RealVNC server module natively for remote graphical desktop access.
Overrides boot procedures targeting specific runlevels. (B1=Console/Requires Login, B2=Console/Autologin, B3=Desktop/Requires Login, B4=Desktop/Autologin).
Injects specific 2-letter regulatory domain codes into wpa_supplicant allowing the WiFi radio to legally initialize.
Automatically pulls the latest commit structural updates forcing the raspi-config utility to update itself.
